skill.name: add-backend-tool
skill.description: Guides developers through adding backend OpenAI function-calling tools, including helper functions, tool schemas, handlers, mode restrictions, and safety guardrails.
skill.summary: Add backend OpenAI function-calling tools with handler updates, mode controls, and implementation guardrails.
skill.author: AppleLamps

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer findings for Ruby shell backticks, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and generic example text. The real risk is medium because the skill guides changes to backend agent tools that may expose filesystem, terminal, or network capabilities if implemented without strict permissions.

medium_findings.title: Backend Tool Expansion Can Expose Powerful Capabilities
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE semantic risk: the skill instructs developers to add backend tools and references file, terminal, and web-search helpers. This is legitimate development guidance, but unsafe implementations could expose filesystem, command execution, or network access through agent function calls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced helpers and examples clearly describe powerful backend capabilities. The file also includes guardrails, so the risk is capability exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Access Control Decisions Are Left To Implementers
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE semantic risk: the skill tells users to update mode restrictions and says agent mode has full access. It warns that chat mode should only allow read-only or safe tools, but it does not define enforceable validation rules.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines directly discuss access modes and permission choices. The risk depends on later implementation, so confidence is high for the concern but not for active exploitation.

low_findings.title: Ruby Shell Backtick Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the reported backtick locations are Markdown inline code or fenced code examples. No Ruby shell execution or executable backtick command was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown formatting and Python example blocks. There is no executable Ruby code path in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the reported lines contain the skill description and example parameter descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption call, or password handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not implement or name weak cryptographic algorithms. The static result appears to be a keyword collision.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the reported line describes agent mode access level. No network scanning, host discovery, port probing, or reconnaissance instruction was found at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a mode label and does not direct the model to scan networks. Nearby lines discuss permission restrictions, not reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Backend Helpers Include File, Terminal, And Web Capabilities
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks developers to review helper functions for file access, terminal commands, and web search. These capabilities require strict authorization and input validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly names helpers with filesystem, command, and network implications. It is a capability risk, not evidence of malicious code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Full Agent Mode Access Requires Careful Tool Restrictions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill notes that agent mode has full access and that chat mode should only include safe tools. Incorrect mode configuration could expose privileged functions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The mode guidance is explicit and security-relevant. The skill warns about safety, so this is an implementation risk rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: Adding agent tools can break backend routing or expose unsafe capabilities. This skill gives a structured checklist for helper functions, tool schemas, handlers, and mode restrictions.
actual_capabilities: Identifies where backend function definitions should be added.
Guides creation of helper functions with error handling.
Shows how to connect new tools to WebSocket function handling.
Prompts developers to update agent and chat mode restrictions.
Reminds developers to add timeouts and avoid hardcoded secrets.
limitations: Does not implement the backend tool automatically.
Does not validate runtime permissions or authorization logic.
Does not provide tests for the new backend function.
Assumes the project uses a Python backend with OpenAI function calling.

use_cases.title: Add a read-only backend utility
use_cases.description: Create a safe helper that returns project information without changing files or running commands.

use_cases.title: Extend an agent with operational actions
use_cases.description: Add a backend function for controlled tasks while checking mode restrictions and timeout behavior.

use_cases.title: Review a new tool before release
use_cases.description: Check whether a proposed tool has clear parameters, error handling, and suitable access limits.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a simple tool
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a low-risk backend addition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a read-only backend tool that lists available project resources. Identify each backend file and safety check needed.

prompt_templates.title: Add a backend function
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate implementation with a defined tool purpose.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add a backend function for my agent. Define the helper, tool parameters, invocation handler, and error handling steps.

prompt_templates.title: Restrict tool access by mode
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced permission review before enabling a tool.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review whether my new backend tool should be available in chat mode, agent mode, or both. Explain the access decision.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a tool implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of safety, reliability, and release readiness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit a proposed backend tool for unsafe parameters, missing timeout handling, weak error handling, and mode restriction gaps.

output_examples.input: Add a tool that lists directory contents.
output: A backend change checklist with the helper location, tool definition location, handler update, and access mode recommendation.
A safety note recommending read-only behavior, path limits, and clear error messages.

output_examples.input: Create a tool for git operations.
output: A review plan that separates safe status checks from write operations such as commits or branch changes.
A recommendation to restrict risky operations to agent mode and add command timeouts.

output_examples.input: Review my new web scraping capability.
output: A risk-focused checklist covering allowed domains, request timeouts, response limits, and mode restrictions.
A release note describing what the tool can access and what user approval it requires.

best_practices: Keep tool parameters narrow and validate every value before use.
Default new tools to the least privileged mode that supports the task.
Add timeouts, clear errors, and secret-handling checks before release.
anti_patterns: Do not expose unrestricted terminal commands through a general-purpose tool.
Do not allow chat mode to call tools that can modify files or systems.
Do not hardcode API keys, tokens, passwords, or private endpoints.

faq.question: What kind of projects does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Python backends that use OpenAI function calling and a WebSocket handler for tool calls.

faq.question: Does this skill write production code for me?
faq.answer: It provides implementation guidance and examples. A developer still needs to adapt and test the backend changes.

faq.question: Can it help with read-only tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful for adding safe tools that inspect files, list resources, or return project metadata.

faq.question: Can it help with tools that run commands?
faq.answer: Yes, but command tools need strict validation, timeouts, and mode restrictions before they are enabled.

faq.question: Does it handle secrets safely?
faq.answer: It reminds developers not to hardcode secrets, but it does not enforce secret scanning or runtime secret controls.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it guides backend changes that can expose file, network, or command capabilities.
